title: Forwarding references parameters should be preferred only to forward parameters
description: "Forwarding references" parameters should be used only to forward parametersAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode Smellcppcoreguidelinessuspicioussince-c++11Why is this an issue?More InfoForwarding referencesare a special kind of references that both ignore and preserve thevalue categoryof a function argument, making it possible to forward it by usingstd::forwardorstd::forward_like.Any code using such a reference for any purpose other than forwarding is actually ignoring the rvalue-ness and const-ness of the associated parameter.Noncompliant code exampleclass Registry { std::vector<std::string> names; public: template <typename StringLike> void addName(StringLike&& arg) { names.emplace_back(arg); // Not forwarded } }; void example() { Registry r; std::string name = "ex
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Forwarding references parameters should be preferred only to forward parameters. Use std::forward.
